mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market deterioration triggers recession expectations that compress corporate earnings forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and decreases in consumer price inflation indicate robust consumer demand without pricing pressure, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of observations or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices signal strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative returns in more than 60% of cases when GDPC1 growth falls below 1.0% annually, or the 5-day return correlation drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect rising household confidence and construction sector activity, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months where HOUST increases, or the signal predictive power vanishes after controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly consumer price inflation exceeds 0.4%, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening that raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in more than 60% of high-inflation months, or the threshold effect disappears when FEDFUNDS is already above 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth exceeds 3.5% annually, markets price in economic overheating and impending monetary contraction, which suppresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 65% of high-GDP-growth quarters, or the negative relationship reverses when inflation remains below 2.0%.
